mappings.evidence: Introduces panelists by name ('Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, you are a Democrat...', 'Manfred Weber, you are the president of the European People's Party...', 'Petr Pavel, you were the Czech President...', 'Daiana Fernández Molero...'), asks questions throughout, manages Q&A ('Can we maybe take one more question'), and closes the session ('I think this was a very rich discussion with really a star panel'). German-British journalist framing fits perfectly.

Responds directly when addressed as 'Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez' at turn 0. Describes being a waitress in New York City, elected in 2018, defeating a 20-year Democratic incumbent, running on $15 minimum wage and Medicare expansion — all matching AOC's biography. Also responds to question about why she is at Munich ('your presidential administration tear apart the transatlantic partnership') as a U.S. representative.

Responds when addressed as 'Manfred Weber, you are the president of the European People's Party' at turn 8. References EPP founding figures (Schuman, Adenauer, de Gasperi), mentions expelling Viktor Orbán, references CDU/CSU, DMA/DSA regulation, and European Parliament votes — all consistent with Weber's bio. Self-references 'the boring thing of a Manfred Weber, of a Christian Democrat'.

Responds when addressed as 'Daiana Fernández Molero' at turn 12. Discusses Milei's reforms, Peronism, Argentina's economic cycles, free trade agreements with the US, Venezuelan exiles in Argentina — all consistent with her bio as an Argentine legislator supporting Milei's reforms. Gives the final word at turn 53.

Responds when addressed as 'Petr Pavel, you were the Czech President' at turn 18. Discusses former communist countries' expectations after 1989, checks and balances against populism, Czech defense industry ('one of the most industrialized countries in Europe with a great tradition in defense industry'), and EU defense spending provision — all consistent with his bio as Czech President and former NATO general.

Turn 35: self-introduces as 'Elena Lazar, Director General of the Hellenic Foundation for European Foreign Policy in Athens, Greece' — not on the roster. Turn 39: self-introduces as 'Ava Federle from New York City' — also not on the roster. These are two different audience members (see flags).

Turn 37: self-introduces as 'Liam Burney, the UK Labour Chair of the Business and Trade Committee in the House of Commons' — not on the roster. Turn 57 is a brief 'Thank you' consistent with same audience member.

flags.reason: Turn 35 is a self-introduction as 'Elena Lazar, Director General of the Hellenic Foundation for European Foreign Policy in Athens, Greece' — a distinct audience member not on the roster.
Turn 39 is a self-introduction as 'Ava Federle from New York City' — a different audience member from Elena Lazar, not on the roster.

flags.note: speaker_f contains turns from two distinct audience members: turn 35 is Elena Lazar (Audience Member 1) and turn 39 is Ava Federle (Audience Member 2). These are clearly different people based on their self-introductions.
